header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary:
I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Organization Development & People Operations professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing processes grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable change.
Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve people experience—through human-centered, data-driven approaches.

bullets.text: Upskill HR team in data analytics to audit 300+ confidential leave-of-absence cases, using evidence-based insights to refute cost assumptions
Translate $362K in leave-cost data into executive-ready insights, identifying trends leading to policy redesigns and first aid training
Consult with executive leadership to redesign accountability structures and clarify cross-functional roles in support of a 2-year strategic plan
Develop SOPs, process documentation, digital resource libraries, and workflows for 70+ cross-functional staff, cutting onboarding time by 75%
Manage end-to-end lifecycles for 20+ projects, utilizing feedback analysis to optimize operational strategies and vendor performance
Facilitate listening sessions with 25 global stakeholders, surfacing learning gaps to co-design 3 tailored programs
Deliver training sessions for 600+ participants, increasing program understanding and driving a 78% enrollment rate
Analyzed 2,000+ employee survey responses, identifying trends and key engagement drivers to recommend C-suite actions
Managed SharePoint dashboards for people metrics, Net Promoter Score, and survey insights to drive employee recognition and engagement
Improved employee experience via 5 cross-functional projects in survey development, performance review, and L&D
Led manager effectiveness initiative, translating data into an executive-ready presentation to inform training strategy
Directed a Mergers & Acquisitions project, partnering with Payroll, People Data, and Finance to ensure smooth organizational transformation
Streamlined onboarding processes across 3 global sites; interviewed stakeholders to clarify responsibilities and map new team workflows
Supported 5 cross-functional HR projects in talent development, succession, and Employee Relations, ensuring data accuracy and confidentiality
Standardized immigration and relocation processes in partnership with immigration attorneys and Mobility team, closing gaps and clarifying handoffs for future cases
Managed cross-functional HR operations across the employee lifecycle — spanning recruitment, compensation, and performance management
Coordinated end-to-end international talent acquisition, including job descriptions, postings, and global compensation benchmarking
Drove a 566% increase in applications in 2 months through personalized global outreach and talent engagement
Planned and executed recruiting events and C-suite panels at top 3 universities, engaging 230+ students and generating 80+ applicants
